idea.hook_en: Your crosshair is a swinging pendulum — you don't aim, you ARREST it at the right moment.
idea.hook_ru: Прицел качается на маятнике — вы не целитесь, вы ОСТАНАВЛИВАЕТЕ его в нужный момент.
idea.mechanic: Top-down arena. Crosshair hangs from a virtual ceiling pivot and swings on a physics arc the player can't directly steer. Press-and-hold STOPS the swing while energy drains from a 100-point budget; release fires a shot along the frozen line. 4 drifting targets (orbit, bounce, linear-h, linear-v), 3 stops per round. Apex hits on movers refund energy.
idea.why_novel: No shooter on Poki/CrazyGames takes control AWAY from the crosshair. Inverting the verb 'aim' into 'commit to a pause' is genre-foreign.
idea.lose_condition: Run out of stops or energy with targets still alive
idea.scope: single-session prototype, one round of 4 targets

sources.summary: LLM-synthesised idea #2 from the 2026-05-13 market scan — combines BPM rhythm-precision verbs with orbital motion mechanics from Hivebound/Sticky Orbit.

synthesizer_brief: BPM rhythm games and orbital trajectory games both reward timing-on-arcs, but they keep player agency on the cursor. Pendulum Sniper inverts that: the crosshair is the part you DON'T control. The verb shifts from aim-and-fire to wait-and-commit. The energy budget makes commitment expensive, and the apex-bonus makes the pendulum's own physics teach the player when to commit.
